Semantic Rule Case ID Feature Overall Coverage 详细信息
SR-MR-001-01Coverage: fully_covered | Req positivenegative|Present negativepositive|Accept negativepositive
SR-MR-001-01TC-SR-MR-001-01-positive-01Capitalised Terms Definitionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-01-positive-01
Title: Capitalised term uses LME Rulebook definition
Intent: Verify that capitalised terms not defined herein correctly use meanings ascribed in the LME Rulebook
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-01
Given
  • a capitalised term is used in the document
  • the term is not otherwise defined herein
When
  • the term is interpreted according to the LME Rulebook
Then
  • the term has the meaning ascribed to it in the LME Rulebook
Assumptions
  • The LME Rulebook contains the definition for the capitalised term
Evidence
MR-001-01: Capitalised terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the Rules and Regulations of the LME (p.2)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote matches atomic rule MR-001-01 exactly.
Underlying semantic rule has low confidence (0.65) and is flagged as first-pass generation; test design relies on stable atomic rule text.
SR-MR-001-01TC-SR-MR-001-01-negative-01Capitalised Terms Definitionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-01-negative-01
Title: Capitalised term uses non-LME Rulebook definition
Intent: Verify that capitalised terms must not use definitions outside the LME Rulebook when not defined herein
Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-01
Given
  • a capitalised term is used in the document
  • the term is not otherwise defined herein
When
  • the term is assigned a meaning different from the LME Rulebook definition
Then
  • the term definition is non-compliant with the LME Rulebook requirement
Assumptions
  • The LME Rulebook contains a definition for the capitalised term
Evidence
MR-001-01: Capitalised terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the meaning ascribed to them in the Rules and Regulations of the LME (p.2)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote matches atomic rule MR-001-01 exactly.
Negative scenario appropriately validates the constraint implied by the obligation.
SR-MR-001-02Coverage: fully_covered | Req positive|Present positive|Accept positive
SR-MR-001-02TC-SR-MR-001-02-positive-01Matching Rules Adoptionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-001-02-positive-01
Title: Exchange adopts matching rules as Administrative Procedures
Intent: Verify that the Exchange may adopt matching rules as part of Administrative Procedures in accordance with the LME Rulebook
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-001-02
Given
  • the Exchange operates under the LME Rulebook
When
  • the Exchange adopts matching rules as part of Administrative Procedures
Then
  • the matching rules are validly adopted in accordance with the LME Rulebook
Assumptions
  • The adoption follows the procedures set out in the LME Rulebook
Evidence
MR-001-02: These matching rules form part of the Administrative Procedures that may be adopted by the Exchange (p.2)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-003-01-positive-01
Title: Member contacts Exchange after price validation failure
Intent: Verify that Members fulfill their obligation to contact the Exchange when an order or trade fails price validation
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-003-01
Given
  • an order or trade fails the price validation check
When
  • the Member contacts the Exchange to explain the rationale as to the price of the rejected order or trade
Then
  • the Member has fulfilled the obligation to explain the price rationale
Assumptions
  • The Member has access to contact the Exchange
  • The price validation check has been performed
Evidence
MR-003-01: Where an order or trade fails the price validation check, Members are required to contact the Exchange (p.2)

Case Type Accepted: True

Title: Extension Request Granted in Exceptional Circumstances
Intent: Verify that requests for extensions to the TOM matching deadline or London inter-office trade input deadline are granted when exceptional circumstances exist.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-004-01
Given
  • A request for extension to the TOM matching deadline or London inter-office trade input deadline is submitted
  • Exceptional circumstances exist justifying the extension
When
  • The extension request is evaluated by the Exchange
Then
  • The extension request is granted
Assumptions
  • The definition of 'exceptional circumstances' is determined by the Exchange on a case-by-case basis
Evidence

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-004-01-boundary-01
Title: Submission at TOM Matching Deadline Boundary
Intent: Verify deadline validation when submission occurs at the TOM matching deadline or London inter-office trade input deadline boundary.
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-004-01
Given
  • A trade is submitted for TOM matching or London inter-office trade input
  • The submission occurs at the deadline boundary
When
  • The deadline validation is performed
Then
  • The deadline validation result is returned as accepted or rejected based on the deadline cutoff
Assumptions
  • The exact time of the TOM matching deadline and London inter-office trade input deadline is defined elsewhere in Exchange rules
  • Boundary timing is evaluated against the specific deadline cutoff time
Evidence
MR-004-01: Requests for extensions to the TOM matching deadline or the London inter-office trade input deadline (p.3)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Scenario tests standard trade submission deadline validation, but semantic rule SR-MR-004-01 defines policy for Extension Requests (exceptional circumstances).
Evidence quote supports extension requests, but scenario steps describe standard trade submission.
Case type 'boundary' is inappropriate for this rule as SR-MR-004-01 defines conditional criteria (exceptional circumstances), not a time boundary (which is covered in SR-MR-004-02).
High hallucination risk: Maker assumed rule defines standard deadline cutoff behavior rather than extension request policy.

  • The system opening time is 01:00

Then
  • The member receives all trade messaging

When

  • Only the current state of the transaction is captured
  • The Trade Mass Status Request is accepted
Assumptions
  • The system provides a Trade Mass Status Request function for members logging in after the open

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-007-01-positive-01
Title: Registered Intermediating Broker submits Agreed Trade before 08:30 deadline
Intent: Verify that Agreed Trade halves agreed during Asian business hours are accepted when submitted before the 08:30 deadline
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-007-01
Given
  • An Agreed Trade half has been agreed during Asian business hours
  • The trade is arranged by a Registered Intermediating Broker
When
  • The Registered Intermediating Broker submits the trade to the Matching System before 08:30 (Europe/London)
Then
  • The trade registration is accepted
  • The deadline validation result is pass
Assumptions
  • Asian business hours are defined per LME Trading Regulations
  • The system correctly identifies trades agreed during Asian business hours
Evidence
MR-007-01: registration of Agreed Trade halves agreed during Asian business hours(including those arranged by a Registered Intermediating Broker) must be submitted to the Matching System by 08:30. (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

SR-MR-008-01TC-SR-MR-008-01-positive-01Member Audit Trail Retentionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-008-01-positive-01
Title: Member retains full audit trail for orders, non-electronic trades, and post-trade operations
Intent: Verify that a member can fulfill their obligation to retain a complete audit trail for all required categories
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-01
Given
  • A member has submitted orders to LME Select
  • The member has Agreed Trades agreed non-electronically
  • The member has performed post-trade operations
When
  • The member retains a full audit trail for all orders, non-electronically agreed trades, and post-trade operations
Then
  • The audit trail is complete and accessible
  • The retention obligation is fulfilled
Assumptions
  • The audit trail includes all required data elements per LME requirements
  • Retention period and format requirements are defined elsewhere in LME regulations
Evidence
MR-008-01: Members must retain a full audit trail for all orders submitted to LME Select, all Agreed Trades agreed non-electronically, and all post-trade operations. (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}

Missing Aspects

Case type is appropriate for verifying obligation fulfillment.
Evidence confirms the obligation but does not detail system-level validation mechanisms.
Test design relies on semantic rule execution mapping for observable outputs (validation_result) which may abstract underlying compliance checks.
Maker evidence is weak regarding how 'retention' is technically verified by the system.

Title: Member fails to retain full audit trail

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-01
Given
  • A member has submitted orders to LME Select
  • The member has Agreed Trades agreed non-electronically
  • The member has performed post-trade operations
When
  • The member does not retain a full audit trail for one or more of the required categories
Then
  • The audit trail is incomplete or missing
  • The member is in non-compliance with the retention obligation
Assumptions
  • Incomplete audit trail may be detected during audits or regulatory reviews
  • Consequences of non-compliance are defined elsewhere in LME regulations
Evidence
MR-008-01: Members must retain a full audit trail for all orders submitted to LME Select, all Agreed Trades agreed non-electronically, and all post-trade operations. (p.4)

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-008-02-positive-01
Title: Audit trail includes USD price and FX rate for non-USD transaction
Intent: Verify that when a Member executes a transaction in a non-USD currency, the audit trail correctly captures the required USD price and FX rate information.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-02
Given
  • A Member has executed a transaction in a currency other than US Dollars
When
  • The audit trail is generated for the transaction
Then
  • The audit trail includes the relevant US Dollars price
  • The audit trail includes the FX rate used to calculate the non-US Dollars price
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-008-02: Where Members have Executed transactions in currencies other than US Dollars, the audit trail for these transactions must include the relevant US Dollars price and the FX rate used to calculate the non-US Dollars price. (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0

Title: Audit trail missing USD price or FX rate for non-USD transaction

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-02

  • A Member has executed a transaction in a currency other than US Dollars
When

Then
  • The audit trail is flagged as incomplete or non-compliant
  • A validation error is raised indicating the missing required fields
Assumptions
  • The system validates audit trail completeness for non-USD transactions

SR-MR-008-02TC-SR-MR-008-02-data_validation-01Non-USD Transaction Audit Trail Requirementsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-008-02-data_validation-01
Title: Validate audit trail data fields for non-USD transaction
Intent: Verify that the audit trail data fields for USD price and FX rate are properly validated for format and consistency.
Priority: high
Case Type: data_validation
Paragraph IDs: MR-008-02
Given
  • A Member has executed a transaction in a currency other than US Dollars
  • The audit trail includes USD price and FX rate fields
When
  • The audit trail data is validated
Then
  • The USD price field contains a valid numeric price value
  • The FX rate field contains a valid numeric exchange rate
  • The non-USD price equals the USD price multiplied by the FX rate within acceptable tolerance
Assumptions
  • FX rate calculation validation is performed by the system
Evidence
MR-008-02: the audit trail for these transactions must include the relevant US Dollars price and the FX rate used to calculate the non-US Dollars price. (p.4)
Case Type: data_validation
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

}

Case type 'data_validation' is appropriate and listed in required case types.
Test extends beyond simple inclusion to verify mathematical consistency (calculation check), which is a strong interpretation of 'FX rate used to calculate'.

Case Type: positive

  • A Member has appropriate documentation in place with their Client
  • The documentation includes required agreements such as give-up agreements
When

Then
  • The order is processed successfully

Evidence

Title: Enter Carry trade with proper audit trail designation

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-01
Given
  • An agreed trade has been designated as a Carry
When
  • The trade is entered into the Matching System as a Carry
  • The audit trail records the trade type designation
Then
  • The audit trail clearly demonstrates the trade is a Carry
  • The trade is distinguishable from an outright trade in the audit trail
Assumptions
  • The Matching System supports Carry trade designation
  • Audit trail captures trade type at entry time

MR-010-01: Agreed trades that are agreed as Carries should be entered as such in to the Matching System (p.4)

Then
  • The audit trail fails to distinguish the trade as a Carry
  • The transaction record is non-compliant with audit trail requirements

SR-MR-010-02TC-SR-MR-010-02-positive-01Carry Trade Leg Submission Ordermissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-010-02-positive-01
Title: Submit Carry trades with legs in correct chronological order
Intent: Verify that Members submit Carry trades with near leg first followed by other legs in chronological prompt date order
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-02
Given
  • A Member is submitting a Carry trade into the Matching System
  • The Carry trade has multiple legs with different prompt dates
When
  • The Member enters the near leg first
  • The Member enters all other legs in chronological prompt date order
Then
  • The Carry trade is accepted by the Matching System
  • The legs are recorded in the correct market convention order
Assumptions
  • Near leg is defined as the leg with the earliest prompt date
  • Chronological prompt date order means ascending date order
Evidence
MR-010-02: When submitting Carry trades into the Matching System, Members must follow market convention (p.4)
MR-010-02: enter the near leg first followed by all other legs in chronological prompt date order (p.4)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-010-02-negative-01
Title: Submit Carry trades with legs in incorrect order
Intent: Verify that submitting Carry trades with legs not in chronological prompt date order is non-compliant
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-010-02
Given
  • A Member is submitting a Carry trade into the Matching System
  • The Carry trade has multiple legs with different prompt dates
When
  • The Member enters legs in an order other than near leg first followed by chronological prompt date order
Then
  • The submission does not follow market convention
  • The leg order is flagged as non-compliant with submission requirements
Assumptions
  • The system can validate leg submission order
  • Incorrect order includes entering far leg before near leg or non-chronological ordering
Evidence
MR-010-02: Members must follow market convention and enter the near leg first (p.4)
MR-010-02: followed by all other legs in chronological prompt date order (p.4)

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-011-01-positive-01
Title: Enter subsequent legs as outright trades after near leg expiration
Intent: Verify that when adjusting a Member to Member Carry trade with expired near leg, subsequent legs are entered as outright trades
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-011-01
Given
  • A previously registered Member to Member Carry trade exists
  • The near leg of the Carry trade has expired
  • There is a requirement to adjust the trade via Reversal or Correction
When
  • The Member enters all subsequent legs to the Matching System as outright trades
Then
  • The subsequent legs are recorded as outright trades
  • The adjustment complies with the requirement for expired near leg Carry trades
Assumptions
  • Subsequent legs refer to legs with prompt dates after the expired near leg
  • Outright trade designation is available in the Matching System
Evidence
MR-011-01: where there is a requirement to adjust(Reversal/Correction) a previously registered Member to Member Carry trade (p.5)
MR-011-01: when the near leg has expired all subsequent legs should be entered to the Matching System as outright trades (p.5)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-011-01-negative-01
Title: Fail to enter subsequent legs as outright trades after near leg expiration
Intent: Verify that failing to enter subsequent legs as outright trades when adjusting an expired near leg Carry trade is non-compliant
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-011-01
Given
  • A previously registered Member to Member Carry trade exists
  • The near leg of the Carry trade has expired
  • There is a requirement to adjust the trade via Reversal or Correction
When
  • The Member enters subsequent legs to the Matching System without designating them as outright trades
Then
  • The subsequent legs are not properly designated as outright trades
  • The adjustment is non-compliant with the requirement for expired near leg Carry trades
Assumptions
  • The system can detect when subsequent legs are not entered as outright trades
  • Entering as Carry instead of outright would be non-compliant
Evidence
MR-011-01: when the near leg has expired all subsequent legs should be entered to the Matching System as outright trades (p.5)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-012-01-positive-01
Title: Member uses LME Base Service for legitimate non-abusive purposes
Intent: Verify that members are permitted to use the LME Base Service for legitimate business activities that do not constitute abusive practices
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-012-01
Given
  • A member is registered to use the LME Base Service
  • The member intends to conduct legitimate trading activities
When
  • The member uses the LME Base Service for compliant trading activities that do not involve avoidance or evasion of Rules or Administrative Procedures
Then
  • The member's use of the LME Base Service is permitted
  • The member's trading activities proceed without rejection
Assumptions
  • The positive case infers permitted behavior from the prohibition text - members may use the LME Base Service for any purpose that is not an abusive practice as defined by the rule
Evidence
MR-012-01: Members must not use the LME Base Service for any abusive practice (p.5)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-012-01-negative-01
Title: Member uses LME Base Service for abusive practice
Intent: Verify that members are prohibited from using the LME Base Service for abusive practices including avoidance or evasion of Rules
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-012-01
Given
  • A member is registered to use the LME Base Service
  • The member attempts to use the service for an abusive practice
When
  • The member uses the LME Base Service for avoidance or evasion of the Rules or any Administrative Procedure
Then
  • The action is rejected
  • The member is not permitted to proceed with the abusive practice
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-012-01: avoidance or evasion of the Rules or any Administrative Procedure (p.5)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True

  • A member is entering a trade to the Matching System
  • The member has their own order/trade data available

Then

  • The trade is processed successfully in the Matching System

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-013-01-negative-01
Title: Member enters trade relying on other party's data
Intent: Verify that members must not rely on trade data entered by the other party to the trade
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-013-01
Given
  • A member is entering a trade to the Matching System
  • The other party to the trade has already entered their trade data
When
  • The member enters the trade to the Matching System relying upon data entered by the other party to the trade
Then
  • The trade entry is flagged as non-compliant
  • The member is reminded of their obligation to use their own order/trade data
Assumptions
  • The system may not automatically reject such trades but the member is in violation of the obligation
Evidence
MR-013-01: not rely upon that entered by the other party to the trade (p.5)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "test_design_quality": 4
}

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Rule language ('reminded') suggests compliance monitoring rather than hard system rejection; scenario aligns with this nuance by expecting flagging rather than rejection.

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B3-1
Given
  • A clearing member is party to a trade that needs to be given up
  • The trade is eligible for give-up processing
When
  • The clearing member uses the Give-Up Executor/Give-Up Clearer code to effect the give-up of the transaction to another Clearing Member
Then
  • The transaction is successfully given up to the designated Clearing Member
  • The give-up processing completes with a valid result
Assumptions
  • The give-up code is a recognized enum value in the system
  • The receiving Clearing Member is properly configured to accept the give-up

Case Type: positive

  • The intention is to bring the transaction on-Exchange
When

Then
  • The OTC contract is successfully converted to a Cleared Contract
  • The transaction is brought on-Exchange
Assumptions
  • The OTC contract meets eligibility requirements for conversion
  • The OTC Bring-On code is a recognized enum value in the system

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B7-1
Given
  • A member intends to submit an Exception transaction to the Matching System
  • The member has prepared details and rationale for the Exception transaction
When
  • The member contacts the LME Post Trade Operations team at posttradeoperations@lme.com or +44(0)20 7113 8201 in advance
  • The member provides details and rationale for the transaction
  • The member submits the Exception transaction to the Matching System
Then
  • The Exception transaction is accepted for processing
Assumptions
  • The advance contact requirement is fulfilled before submission
  • The LME Post Trade Operations team acknowledges the contact

Title: Transfer registered trade between Members within same business day

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-014-B9-1
Given
  • A registered single trade or group of trades exists
  • The trade is ready to be transferred between Members
When
  • The member submits the transfer request within the same business day (business_day_offset 0)
Then
  • The transfer is processed successfully
  • The position is moved between Members
Assumptions
  • Same business day submission meets the deadline requirement based on business_day_offset of 0

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-017-B4-1-boundary-01
Title: Previous Business Day Boundary for YS Code
Intent: Verify the boundary condition when transitioning between business days for Yesterday's Settlement Price code
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-017-B4-1
Given
  • A trade requires Yesterday's Settlement Price YS code
  • The current business day is Day T
  • The previous business day is Day T-1
When
  • The price is calculated using the YS code at the business day boundary
Then
  • The price correctly references the previous Business Day's relevant Settlement Price
  • The deadline validation result is accepted
Assumptions
  • Boundary is inferred from 'previous Business Day' reference in evidence
  • No explicit numeric boundary value is specified in the evidence
  • Business day calendar is defined and available
Evidence
MR-017-B4-1: Yesterday's Settlement Price("YS") Code – where the price is the previous Business Day's relevant Settlement Price (p.7)

Title: Substituted Absolute Price Value Validation

Case Type: data_validation
Paragraph IDs: MR-018-01
Given
  • A trade has been submitted using a short price code
  • The relevant absolute value has been made available
  • The Matching System has performed the substitution
When
  • The substituted price values are validated
Then
  • The substituted absolute price matches the published relevant absolute value
  • All leg prices are populated with valid absolute values
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-018-01: the Matching System will substitute the correct price for each code (p.8)
Case Type: data_validation
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-022-01-positive-01
Title: Clearing Member approves trade within 10-minute deadline
Intent: Verify that a Clearing Member can successfully approve or reject an Agreed Trade within the required 10-minute window after RIB registration
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-022-01
Given
  • A RIB has registered an Agreed Trade in the Matching System
  • The registration timestamp is recorded
When
  • The Clearing Member approves or rejects the Agreed Trade within 10 minutes of such registration
Then
  • The deadline validation result equals pass
  • The approval or rejection is accepted by the Matching System
Assumptions
  • The 10-minute window is calculated from the exact registration timestamp
  • The Matching System accurately records the registration time
Evidence
MR-022-01: the Clearing Member must approve or reject the Agreed Trade within the Matching System within 10 minutes of such registration (p.9)

SR-MR-022-02TC-SR-MR-022-02-positive-01PTT Order Arrangement Time Determinationmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-022-02-positive-01
Title: Arrangement time correctly set for PTT Order from Pre-Trade Communications
Intent: Verify that for an Agreed Trade comprising a PTT Order, the arrangement time is correctly determined from the RIB's Pre-Trade Communications
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-022-02
Given
  • An Agreed Trade comprises a PTT Order
  • A RIB has Pre-Trade Communications in respect of two Clients
  • The two Clients would comprise an IOM PTT Initiating Pair subject to acceptance by the Clearing Member(s)
When
  • The time of arrangement is determined for the PTT Order
Then
  • The time of arrangement is set to the time from which the RIB has Pre-Trade Communications in respect of the two Clients
Assumptions
  • The Pre-Trade Communications timestamp is accurately recorded by the system
  • The two Clients meet the criteria for an IOM PTT Initiating Pair
Evidence
MR-022-02: where the Agreed Trade comprises a PTT Order, the time of "arrangement" shall be the time from which the RIB has Pre-Trade Communications in respect of two Clients (p.9)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0

Strong alignment between scenario data ('R 1', Ring session) and rule evidence quote.
Evidence explicitly supports the specific value constraint tested.

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-023-B3-1
Given

  • The trade was executed during a Ring session
When
  • The trade time field is entered as "X 1" (an invalid indicator)
Then
  • The trade time field is rejected
  • A validation error is returned indicating invalid trade time format
Assumptions
  • The negative case tests the violation of the allowed values constraint for Ring venue trade time

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-D-1-negative-01
Title: Submit trade with unsupported account type
Intent: Verify that the Matching System rejects a trade with an unsupported account type
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-D-1
Given
  • A Clearing Member is submitting a trade for matching
  • The trade requires an account designation
When
  • The trade is submitted with an unsupported account type (e.g., "X")
Then
  • The account type is rejected
  • A validation error is returned indicating the account type is not supported
Assumptions
  • The negative case tests the violation of the supported account types enumeration
  • Only H, U, C, G, S are supported as per the evidence

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-E-1-positive-01
Title: Agreed Trade is Executed When All Acceptance Criteria and Pre-Execution Checks Are Satisfied
Intent: Verify that an Agreed Trade is executed when it satisfies the Acceptance Criteria, is matched by the Matching System, and all Pre-Execution Checks are satisfied following Fixed Price Auction completion.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-E-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade has been submitted
  • Any Fixed Price Auction has completed
  • The Agreed Trade satisfies the Acceptance Criteria
  • All Pre-Execution Checks are satisfied
When
  • The Matching System confirms that the Agreed Trade is matched
Then
  • The Agreed Trade is Executed
Assumptions
  • Execution timing is determined by the Matching System confirmation
  • The Acceptance Criteria and Pre-Execution Checks are defined elsewhere in the LME Rules
Evidence
MR-024-E-1: the Agreed Trade shall, provided that it satisfies the Acceptance Criteria, be Executed at the time, following the completion of any Fixed Price Auction, that the Matching System confirms that the Agreed Trade is matched... (p.9)

Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-024-E-1-negative-01
Title: Agreed Trade is Not Executed When Acceptance Criteria Are Not Satisfied
Intent: Verify that an Agreed Trade is not executed when it fails to satisfy the Acceptance Criteria, even if other conditions are met.
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-024-E-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade has been submitted
  • Any Fixed Price Auction has completed
  • The Agreed Trade does NOT satisfy the Acceptance Criteria
When
  • The Matching System processes the Agreed Trade
Then
  • The Agreed Trade is NOT Executed
Assumptions
  • The Matching System will not confirm a match when Acceptance Criteria are not satisfied
Evidence
MR-024-E-1: the Agreed Trade shall, provided that it satisfies the Acceptance Criteria, be Executed (p.9)

Title: Client Contract Field is Populated With Beneficial Owner Code From Member Records

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-025-01
Given
  • A Client Contract is being created or updated
  • The Member has a code identifying the beneficial owner in their books and records
When
  • The Member populates the Client Contract field with the beneficial owner identification code
Then
  • The field is populated with the code used to identify the beneficial owner of the Client Contract in the Member's books and records
Assumptions
  • The Member maintains consistent codes for beneficial owners in their books and records
Evidence

  • The same beneficial owner has positions reported to the Exchange
When
  • The Member uses the same code for the Client Contract as used when reporting positions to the Exchange
Then
  • The code is accepted as consistent with Exchange position reporting

  • The same beneficial owner has positions reported to the Exchange using a different code
When

Then

  • The system can detect inconsistency between Client Contract codes and position reporting codes
  • The same beneficial owner should have the same code across both contexts

Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-026-01
Given
  • A Member is entering orders on LMEselect
  • Account identification is appropriate for the order type
When
  • The Member identifies the correct account for the order
Then
  • The order is accepted with the correct account identification
Assumptions
  • The phrase 'where appropriate' implies certain order types or situations may not require account identification
  • The correct account is determined by the Member's internal processes and LME requirements
Evidence

Then
  • The order is rejected or an error is flagged for incorrect account identification
Assumptions
  • The system validates account identification for orders where it is appropriate
  • Incorrect account identification includes both missing and wrong account references

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-028-01-positive-01
Title: Clearing Member Uses Transfer Category to Move Trades
Intent: Verify that a clearing member can use the transfer trade category to move trades between Members or within their own account structure
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-028-01
Given
  • A clearing member has a single trade or a group of trades (a position) to move
  • The transfer involves either a common Client or non-common Client between two Clearing Members or within the Member's own account structure
When
  • The clearing member uses the transfer trade category code to move the trade(s)
Then
  • The trade category is accepted
  • The position is moved to the target account or Member
Assumptions
  • The submission meets the business day deadline requirement
  • All required trade and account information is provided
Evidence
MR-028-01: This post-trade category should be used to move a single trade or a group of trades(“a position”) between Members (p.10)
MR-028-01: for a common Client or non-common Client between two Clearing Members or alternatively within a Member's own account structure (p.10)

Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-028-01-boundary-01
Title: Transfer Category Submission at Business Day Cutoff
Intent: Verify deadline validation when transfer category submission occurs at the business day cutoff boundary
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-028-01
Given
  • A clearing member has a trade or position to transfer
  • The submission is prepared for the transfer trade category
When
  • The clearing member submits the transfer category request at the business day cutoff (business_day_offset: 0)
Then
  • The deadline validation result is accepted
Assumptions
  • Business day offset of 0 indicates same-day submission deadline
  • The deadline_kind 'business_day_cutoff' defines the end of the business day as the boundary
Evidence
MR-028-01: This post-trade category should be used to move a single trade or a group of trades(“a position”) between Members (p.10)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote provided does not explicitly support the business day cutoff boundary condition; it only describes trade category usage. The boundary condition relies on inferred semantic rule classification rather than explicit text evidence.

Title: Late Transfer Category Submission Rejected

  • The business day cutoff has passed
When
  • The clearing member submits the transfer category request after the deadline
Then
  • The submission is flagged as late submission
  • The deadline validation result indicates breach

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-030-03-positive-01
Title: Member confirms all required client details via agreement
Intent: Verify that a member properly confirms client status and instructions via the agreement for metal swap transactions
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-030-03
Given
  • A member is processing a metal swap transaction for a client
When
  • The member confirms via the agreement that the client is a direct customer
  • The member confirms via the agreement that the client is a commercial user of the metal concerned
  • The member confirms via the agreement that the client has given written instructions regarding the metal swap transaction
Then
  • All required client confirmations are recorded in the agreement
  • The transaction proceeds with proper client verification
Assumptions
  • The agreement provides a mechanism for recording all three required confirmations
Evidence
MR-030-03: Each Member must confirm via the agreement that the Client is a direct customer, is a commercial user of the metal concerned and had given the Member written instructions regarding the metal swap transaction. (p.11)

Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-033-02-boundary-01
Title: Minimum trades required for weighted average calculation
Intent: Test the boundary between single trade transfer and weighted average transfer
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-033-02
Given
  • A member has exactly two trades of the same direction (long or short)
  • Both trades use the same pricing basis
When
  • The member transfers using a weighted average of the two trades with proper audit trail
Then
  • The weighted average transfer is accepted as the minimum threshold for averaging
Assumptions
  • The boundary between single trade and weighted average is inferred as the minimum number of trades (2) required to calculate an average
  • No explicit boundary value is specified in the evidence; this scenario tests the conceptual transition point
Evidence
MR-033-02: Members may transfer a single trade or a trade weighted average (p.11)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-033-03-positive-01
Title: Member transfers positions within same account
Intent: Verify that members can transfer positions legitimately within a single account without offsetting between accounts
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-033-03
Given
  • A member has positions within a single account
When
  • The member transfers or offsets positions within that same account
Then
  • The transfer is accepted
  • The position transfer is processed successfully
Assumptions
  • The permitted action is inferred as the inverse of the prohibition: members may transfer or offset positions within a single account, or transfer positions between accounts without using offsetting for evasion purposes
  • The evidence describes only what is prohibited; the permitted action is inferred
Evidence
MR-033-03: Members may not offset long and short positions between two or more accounts (p.11)

Title: Clearing Member offsets net position at Official Settlement Price

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-034-01
Given
  • A Clearing Member has a net position to offset
  • The Official Settlement Price for the Prompt Date is available
When
  • The Clearing Member submits the relevant entries to the Matching System priced at the Official Settlement Price for the Prompt Date
Then
  • The entries are accepted by the Matching System
  • The net position is offset at the Official Settlement Price
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-034-01: a net position may be offset at each Clearing Member by submitted the relevant entries to the Matching System priced at the Official Settlement Price for the Prompt Date (p.12)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-035-01-positive-01
Title: Use Transfer trade category to offset onExchange position with OTC position for common Client
Intent: Verify that the Transfer post-trade category may be used to offset an existing onExchange position at one Member with an OTC position at another Member for a common Client
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-035-01
Given
  • An existing onExchange position exists at one Member for a Client
  • An OTC position exists at another Member for the same Client
When
  • A trade is submitted using the Transfer trade category in conjunction with the OTC Bring-On trade category
Then
  • The Transfer trade category is accepted for offsetting the positions
Assumptions
  • The OTC Bring-On trade category is applied as applicable per the rule
Evidence
MR-035-01: The Transfer post-trade category may also be used to offset an existing onExchange position at one Member with an OTC position at another Member for a common Client (p.12)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-036-01
Given
  • A trade has been submitted to the Matching System
  • One or more fields contain errors from the point of trade submission
When
  • A Reversal/Correction is executed using the cancellation trade flag to correct the errors
Then
  • The Reversal/Correction is processed to correct the field errors
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-036-01: To execute a Reversal/Correction the cancellation trade flag should be used to correct errors that may have occurred in one or more of the fields entered into the Matching System (p.12)

Case Type: positive

  • A trade was previously submitted with a Reversal/Correction trade category
  • The previously registered trade has a specific trade category
When
  • The trade is submitted with the same trade category as the previously registered trade
Then
  • The trade submission is accepted

MR-037-01: Trades previously submitted with a Reversal/Correction trade category must be submitted with the same trade category as the previously registered trade (p.12)

SR-MR-038-01TC-SR-MR-038-01-positive-01Reversal Trade Economic Details Matchingmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-038-01-positive-01
Title: Submit reversal trade with exact economic details matching original trade
Intent: Verify that a reversal trade submitted with the exact economic trade details of the originally registered trade (other than Buy/Sell indicator) is accepted by the Matching System
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-038-01
Given
  • A trade has been originally registered in the Matching System
  • The original trade has specific economic details including price, quantity, contract month, and other trade attributes
When
  • A trade is submitted to the Matching System with the Reversal trade flag
  • The submitted reversal trade reflects the exact economic trade details of the originally registered trade
  • The Buy/Sell indicator is the only field that differs from the original trade
Then
  • The reversal trade is accepted by the Matching System
  • The reversal trade is processed for matching
Assumptions
  • The original trade details are available for comparison
  • The reversal trade flag is properly set in the submission
Evidence
MR-038-01: Trades submitted to the Matching System with the Reversal trade flag must reflect the exact economic trade details of the trade originally registered other than the 'Buy/Sell' indicator. (p.12)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-038-01-negative-01
Title: Submit reversal trade with mismatched economic details
Intent: Verify that a reversal trade submitted with economic details that do not match the originally registered trade is rejected
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-038-01
Given
  • A trade has been originally registered in the Matching System
  • The original trade has specific economic details including price, quantity, contract month, and other trade attributes
When
  • A trade is submitted to the Matching System with the Reversal trade flag
  • The submitted reversal trade does NOT reflect the exact economic trade details of the originally registered trade
  • One or more economic details (other than Buy/Sell indicator) differ from the original trade
Then
  • The reversal trade is rejected by the Matching System
  • An error is returned indicating mismatch with original trade economic details
Assumptions
  • The system validates reversal trade details against the original registered trade
  • The rejection message identifies the mismatched fields
Evidence
MR-038-01: Trades submitted to the Matching System with the Reversal trade flag must reflect the exact economic trade details of the trade originally registered other than the 'Buy/Sell' indicator. (p.12)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-039-01-positive-01
Title: Cancel and resubmit Client Contract on trade day T
Intent: Verify that a Client Contract may be cancelled and resubmitted to the Matching System on the day of trade (T)
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-039-01
Given
  • A Client Contract has been submitted to the Matching System
  • The current date is the day of trade (T)
When
  • The Client Contract is cancelled
  • The Client Contract is resubmitted to the Matching System on the same day (T)
Then
  • The cancellation is accepted
  • The resubmission of the Client Contract is accepted by the Matching System
Assumptions
  • The actor has appropriate permissions to cancel and resubmit Client Contracts
  • The cancellation and resubmission occur within the same trading day T
Evidence
MR-039-01: Client Contracts may be cancelled and resubmitted to the Matching System on the day of trade("T") but errors identified thereafter(T+1 onwards) must be corrected using the Reversal/Correction above. (p.12)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-040-01-positive-01
Title: Member uses Reversal/Correction process for LME Select price adjustment
Intent: Verify that a Member correctly uses the Reversal/Correction process with Normal trade category against venue inter-office to effect a price adjustment for a trade executed in LME Select
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-040-01
Given
  • A trade has been executed in LME Select
  • The trade requires a price adjustment as a result of the Lending Rules
When
  • A Member uses the Reversal/Correction process
  • The trade category is set to Normal
  • The venue is set to inter-office
Then
  • The price adjustment is effected through the Reversal/Correction process
  • The adjustment is accepted by the Matching System
Assumptions
  • The Lending Rules have triggered a requirement for price adjustment
  • The Member has access to the Reversal/Correction functionality
Evidence
MR-040-01: In the event that a trade Executed in LME Select requires a price adjustment as a result of the Lending Rules, Members must use the Reversal/Correction process Normal trade category against venue inter-office to effect t... (p.12)

Case Type Accepted: True

Then

  • Alternative methods for price adjustment are not permitted for LME Select trades requiring Lending Rules adjustments

Intent: Verify that the RIB successfully liaises with each Clearing Member to agree the correct details of the Agreed Trade to be re-submitted
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B2-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade arranged by a RIB has been matched and submitted to the Matching System
  • The trade needs to go through the Reversal/Correction process
When
  • The RIB liaises with each Clearing Member to agree the correct details of the Agreed Trade to be re-submitted
Then
  • The correct details of the Agreed Trade are agreed between the RIB and each Clearing Member
  • The trade is ready for re-submission
Assumptions 
Evidence

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-042-B2-1-negative-01
Title: RIB Fails to Liaise with Clearing Members Before Re-submission
Intent: Verify that failure to liaise with Clearing Members prevents proper agreement of trade details for re-submission
Priority: medium
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B2-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade arranged by a RIB has been matched and submitted to the Matching System
  • The trade needs to go through the Reversal/Correction process
When
  • The RIB does not liaise with each Clearing Member to agree the correct details before attempting re-submission
Then
  • The correct details of the Agreed Trade are not agreed between the RIB and Clearing Members
  • The re-submission cannot proceed with agreed details
Assumptions
  • The system or process enforces that liaison must occur before re-submission can complete
Evidence
MR-042-B2-1: the RIB must liaise with each Clearing Member to agree the correct details of the Agreed Trade to be re-submitted; (p.13)

Findings
Testability risk: Assumption states 'system or process enforces that liaison must occur'. Liaison is often a human/process step; ensure the test oracle can verify enforcement via system logs or status gates rather than relying solely on process compliance.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-042-B3-1-positive-01
Title: RIB Enters Two New Trades with Reversal/Correction Cancellation Flag
Intent: Verify that the RIB correctly enters two new trades with the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction to counter the original registration
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade has been matched and submitted to the Matching System
  • The correct details have been agreed between the RIB and Clearing Members
  • A Reversal/Correction process is required
When
  • The RIB enters two new trades with the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction
  • The first trade counters the original registration of the Agreed Trade with Cancellation flag set to R(Reversal)
Then
  • Two new trades are entered into the Matching System
  • The Cancellation flag is set to Reversal/Correction
  • The original registration of the Agreed Trade is countered
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-042-B3-1: the RIB shall enter two new trades with the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction, to: (i) counter the original registration of the Agreed Trade in the Matching System(Cancellation flag set to R(Reversal)) (p.13)

Title: RIB Enters Trades Without Proper Cancellation Flag Setting

Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-1

  • The correct details have been agreed between the RIB and Clearing Members
  • A Reversal/Correction process is required
When
  • The RIB enters trades without setting the Cancellation flag to Reversal/Correction
Then
  • The Reversal/Correction process is not properly executed
  • The original registration of the Agreed Trade is not countered

  • The system validates that the Cancellation flag must be set to Reversal/Correction for this process

Title: System Error During Reversal/Correction Trade Entry
Intent: Verify handling of exceptional circumstances when the RIB cannot complete trade entry for Reversal/Correction
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-1
Given
  • An Agreed Trade has been matched and submitted to the Matching System
  • The correct details have been agreed between the RIB and Clearing Members
  • A Reversal/Correction process is required
When
  • The RIB attempts to enter two new trades with the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction
  • A system error or unexpected condition prevents trade entry completion
Then
  • The Reversal/Correction trade entry fails
  • An appropriate error or exception is raised
  • The original trade registration remains unchanged
Assumptions
  • The system has error handling for trade entry failures during Reversal/Correction process

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-042-B3-3-negative-01
Title: Clearing Member enters trades without proper Cancellation flag for Reversal/Correction
Intent: Verify that trades entered without the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction do not counter the original registration
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-042-B3-3
Given
  • An Agreed Trade has been registered in the Matching System
  • The Clearing Member needs to counter the original registration
When
  • The Clearing Member enters two new trades without the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction
Then
  • The trades are not processed as reversal/correction trades
  • The original registration of the Agreed Trade is not countered
Assumptions
  • The system validates the Cancellation flag value for reversal/correction processing
Evidence
MR-042-B3-3: each Clearing Member shall enter two new trades with the Cancellation flag set to Reversal/Correction (p.13)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

SR-MR-060-A-1TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-positive-01UNA Mnemonic Non-Give-Up Usage Compliancemissingnot_applicable
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-positive-01
Title: Member uses UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts registration with full compliance
Intent: Verify that a Member can use the UNA mnemonic for purposes other than Give-Up registration when all compliance conditions are met
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A Member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts registration
  • The Member has obligations under Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
  • The Member has appropriate position-keeping accounts at the Clearing House
When
  • The Member uses the UNA mnemonic in connection with the registration of Client Contracts
  • The Member complies with obligations under Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
  • The Member allocates positions to the relevant position-keeping account at the Clearing House
  • The Member ensures positions are allocated to appropriate accounts for legal/regulatory compliance
Then
  • The use of the UNA mnemonic is permitted
  • The registration proceeds successfully
Assumptions
  • The Member has verified that such use is permissible under the broader Matching Rules and LME Rulebook
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: Such use of the "UNA" mnemonic is permitted, provided that (p.13)
MR-060-A-1: Members comply with their obligations under Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations (p.13)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores

  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Semantic rule is classified as 'reference_only' and 'non_testable'.
Required case types list is empty; test case creation is inappropriate for this rule.

SR-MR-060-A-1TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-negative-01UNA Mnemonic Non-Give-Up Usage Compliancemissingnot_applicable
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-negative-01
Title: Member uses UNA mnemonic without complying with Regulation 3.3 obligations
Intent: Verify that use of UNA mnemonic is not permitted when the Member fails to comply with Regulation 3.3 obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A Member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts registration
  • The Member has obligations under Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
When
  • The Member uses the UNA mnemonic without complying with obligations under Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations
Then
  • The use of the UNA mnemonic is not permitted
  • The registration is rejected or flagged for compliance review
Assumptions
  • The system can validate compliance with Regulation 3.3 obligations
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: Such use of the "UNA" mnemonic is permitted, provided that: (a) Members comply with their obligations under Regulation 3.3 (p.13)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Semantic rule SR-MR-060-A-1 is classified as 'reference_only' and 'non_testable' with 'coverage_eligible: false'.
Designing a functional negative test case for a non-testable reference rule implies system enforcement not supported by the rule classification.
Evidence quote supports the condition but does not justify system-level rejection logic.
Maker evidence is weak regarding system behavior; rule text describes permission conditions, not system validation logic.
SR-MR-060-A-1TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-exception-01UNA Mnemonic Non-Give-Up Usage Compliancemissingnot_applicable
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-A-1-exception-01
Title: Member cannot allocate positions to appropriate account due to Clearing House system unavailability
Intent: Verify handling when a Member is unable to fulfill position allocation responsibilities due to exceptional circumstances beyond their control
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-A-1
Given
  • A Member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts registration
  • The Member is ready to allocate positions to appropriate accounts
  • The Clearing House position-keeping system is temporarily unavailable
When
  • The Member attempts to use the UNA mnemonic and allocate positions
  • The Clearing House system is unavailable for position allocation
Then
  • The system handles the exception appropriately
  • The Member is notified of the inability to complete position allocation
  • The trade is held in a pending state or rejected with appropriate error message
Assumptions
  • The system has exception handling for Clearing House unavailability
  • The Member should not be penalized for system unavailability beyond their control
Evidence
MR-060-A-1: it is the sole responsibility of the Member to ensure that it allocates positions in respect of Contracts to an appropriate position-keeping account at the Clearing House (p.13)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 1,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 1
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Semantic rule SR-MR-060-A-1 is 'reference_only' and 'non_testable'.
Evidence quote refers to Member responsibility ('sole responsibility of the Member'), not system exception handling for Clearing House unavailability.
High hallucination risk: Test assumes system pending states and error messages not defined in the rule or evidence.
Case type 'exception' is not supported by the rule structure.
Maker evidence is weak; no source text supports the claimed system exception handling behavior.

SR-MR-060-B-1TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-positive-01UNA Mnemonic Position Allocation Responsibilitymissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-positive-01
Title: Member allocates positions to appropriate position-keeping account enabling client compliance
Intent: Verify that a Member can successfully fulfill their responsibility to allocate positions to appropriate accounts for client compliance
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-B-1
Given
  • A Member is using the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts
  • The Member has clients with applicable legal, regulatory, or contractual requirements
When
  • The Member allocates positions in respect of Contracts to an appropriate position-keeping account at the Clearing House
  • The allocation enables the Member and its Clients to comply with applicable legal or regulatory requirements and contractual obligations
Then
  • The position allocation is accepted
  • The Member and Clients are enabled to comply with applicable requirements
Assumptions
  • The Member has identified the appropriate position-keeping account for each client's requirements
Evidence
MR-060-B-1: it is the sole responsibility of the Member to ensure that it allocates positions in respect of Contracts to an appropriate position-keeping account (p.13)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

SR-MR-060-B-1TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-negative-01UNA Mnemonic Position Allocation Responsibilitymissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-negative-01
Title: Member allocates positions to inappropriate account failing to enable client compliance
Intent: Verify that the Member's failure to allocate positions to appropriate accounts is identified as non-compliant
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-B-1
Given
  • A Member is using the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts
  • The Member has clients with applicable legal, regulatory, or contractual requirements
When
  • The Member allocates positions to an inappropriate position-keeping account
  • The allocation does not enable the Member and its Clients to comply with applicable requirements
Then
  • The position allocation is flagged as non-compliant
  • The Member is responsible for the compliance failure
Assumptions
  • The system or compliance monitoring can detect inappropriate account allocations
Evidence
MR-060-B-1: it is the sole responsibility of the Member to ensure that it allocates positions in respect of Contracts to an appropriate position-keeping account (p.13)

Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4

Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Semantic rule SR-MR-060-B-1 is 'testable'.
Evidence cites Member responsibility; test assumes system flagging of non-compliance.
While acceptable for a workflow rule, the distinction between manual Member responsibility and automated system flagging should be verified.
Case type 'negative' is appropriate for verifying compliance constraints.
Requirement coverage is solid but relies on assumption of automated enforcement.
SR-MR-060-B-1TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-exception-01UNA Mnemonic Position Allocation Responsibilitymissinguncovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-060-B-1-exception-01
Title: Member faces conflicting legal or regulatory requirements for position allocation
Intent: Verify handling when a Member cannot satisfy all applicable legal or regulatory requirements due to conflicting obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-B-1
Given
  • A Member is using the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts
  • The Member has clients subject to multiple jurisdictions with conflicting requirements
When
  • The Member attempts to allocate positions to comply with applicable requirements
  • Conflicting legal or regulatory requirements prevent a single appropriate allocation
Then
  • The system flags the conflict for manual review
  • The Member is notified of the conflicting requirements
  • An exception handling process is initiated
Assumptions
  • The system has a process for handling conflicting regulatory requirements
  • Manual intervention may be required to resolve the conflict
Evidence
MR-060-B-1: to enable the Member and its Client(s) to comply with any applicable legal or regulatory requirements and/or any contractual obligations (p.13)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: not_relevant
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: True
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 1,
  "requirement_coverage": 1,
  "test_design_quality": 2
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence inconsistency: Scenario evidence quote ('to enable the Member...') does not match the semantic rule evidence for SR-MR-060-B-1 ('60. The OTC Bring-On category...').
Logic mismatch: Scenario describes UNA mnemonic position allocation conflicts (SR-MR-060-C-1 logic), not OTC Bring-On category eligibility (SR-MR-060-B-1 logic).
Hallucination risk: High. Maker appears to have applied C-1 rule content to B-1 case ID.

Intent: Verify that a Member can use the UNA mnemonic for purposes other than Give-Up registration when all conditions are met
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-060-C-1

  • A Member intends to use the UNA mnemonic for Client Contracts registration
  • The use is not in connection with the facilitation of Give-Up trades

  • The Member uses the UNA mnemonic
  • The Member complies with Regulation 3.3 of the Trading Regulations

  • The Member allocates positions to an appropriate position-keeping account

  • The use of the UNA mnemonic is permitted
  • The transaction proceeds without rejection

  • The Member has verified that the use case qualifies as a non-Give-Up purpose
  • All regulatory and clearing house requirements are satisfied

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-A-1-positive-01
Title: Seller transfers commodity to single counterparty under financing agreement
Intent: Verify that a financing transaction is accepted when the seller transfers or pledges a commodity to only one counterparty
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-A-1
Given
  • A transaction governed by an agreement to transfer commodities exists
  • The seller holds a particular commodity for transfer or pledge
When
  • The seller transfers or pledges the commodity to exactly one counterparty
Then
  • The transaction is accepted
  • The transfer or pledge is recorded as valid
Assumptions
  • The agreement to transfer commodities is properly documented
  • The commodity is not already pledged to another counterparty
Evidence
MR-044-A-1: the seller can only transfer or pledge a particular commodity to one counterparty at a time (p.14)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-A-1-negative-01
Title: Seller attempts to transfer same commodity to multiple counterparties
Intent: Verify that a financing transaction is rejected when the seller attempts to transfer or pledge the same commodity to multiple counterparties simultaneously
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-A-1
Given
  • A transaction governed by an agreement to transfer commodities exists
  • The seller has already transferred or pledged a particular commodity to one counterparty
When
  • The seller attempts to transfer or pledge the same commodity to another counterparty
Then
  • The transaction is rejected
  • An error indicates the commodity is already pledged to another counterparty
Assumptions
  • The system maintains a record of existing pledges or transfers
  • Duplicate pledge detection is performed during transaction validation
Evidence
MR-044-A-1: the seller can only transfer or pledge a particular commodity to one counterparty at a time (p.14)

Title: Seller agrees to repurchase commodities or substituted commodities
Intent: Verify that a financing transaction is accepted when the seller agrees to repurchase the commodities or substituted commodities of the same description
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-A-2
Given
  • A financing transaction governed by an agreement to transfer commodities exists
  • The seller has transferred commodities to the counterparty
When
  • The seller agrees to repurchase the same commodities
  • Or the seller agrees to repurchase substituted commodities of the same description
Then
  • The transaction is accepted
  • The repurchase agreement is recorded as valid
Assumptions
  • The repurchase agreement is documented as part of the transaction
  • Substituted commodities meet the same description criteria
Evidence
MR-044-A-2: the seller must agree to repurchase them, or substituted commodities of the same description (p.14)

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-A-2

  • The seller does not include a repurchase agreement
When
  • The seller does not agree to repurchase the commodities
  • The seller does not agree to provide substituted commodities of the same description
Then
  • The transaction is rejected
  • An error indicates the repurchase agreement is required
Assumptions
  • The repurchase agreement is a mandatory element of financing transactions
  • The system validates for the presence of repurchase terms
Evidence
MR-044-A-2: the seller must agree to repurchase them, or substituted commodities of the same description (p.14)

  • The seller has agreed to repurchase the commodities
When
  • The seller agrees to a specified repurchase price
  • The seller agrees to a specific future date for repurchase
Then
  • The transaction is accepted
  • The repurchase terms with price and date are recorded as valid
Assumptions
  • The specified price is clearly documented in the agreement
  • The specific future date is defined and valid

  • The seller has agreed to repurchase the commodities
When
  • The seller does not specify a repurchase price
  • Or the seller does not specify a specific future date for repurchase
Then
  • The transaction is rejected

SR-MR-044-C-1TC-SR-MR-044-C-1-positive-01Financing Trade Category for Repurchase Transactionsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-C-1-positive-01
Title: Financing trade category used for valid repurchase transaction
Intent: Verify that a repurchase transaction meeting all specified criteria can use the Financing trade category without triggering transaction reporting obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-C-1
Given
  • A repurchase transaction where a seller sells a commodity and agrees to repurchase it in the future
  • The transaction is governed by an agreement to transfer commodities
  • The seller transfers or pledges a particular commodity to only one counterparty
  • The seller agrees to repurchase the commodities at a specified price on a specific future date
When
  • The Financing trade category is assigned to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction does not result in any transaction reporting obligations under MiFID II
Assumptions
  • The transaction price is likely to be away from the prevailing current market price as stated in the rule
Evidence
MR-044-C-1: a repurchase transaction – involves selling a commodity and agreeing to repurchase it in the future (p.14)
MR-044-C-1: securities financing transactions are not considered to be reportable transactions (p.14)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Case type 'positive' is appropriate for the workflow rule.
Scenario validates the exemption from reporting obligations when criteria are met.
Note: Rule SR-MR-044-C-1 requires 'exception' case type which is not present in this batch.
SR-MR-044-C-1TC-SR-MR-044-C-1-negative-01Financing Trade Category for Repurchase Transactionsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-C-1-negative-01
Title: Financing trade category used for non-qualifying transaction
Intent: Verify that a transaction not meeting any of the specified financing criteria cannot use the Financing trade category to avoid reporting obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-C-1
Given
  • A transaction that does not meet criteria (a), (b), (c), or (d) for financing transactions
  • The transaction is not a repurchase, securities lending, buy-sell back, or margin lending transaction
When
  • The Financing trade category is assigned to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction is not exempt from transaction reporting obligations under MiFID II
  • The use of Financing trade category does not prevent transaction reporting requirements
Assumptions
  • The system validates whether the transaction meets the financing criteria before exempting from reporting
Evidence
MR-044-C-1: the use of the Financing trade category will not result in any transaction reporting obligations, provided the transaction itself meets the following criteria (p.14)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Case type 'negative' is appropriate for testing non-qualifying transactions.
Scenario correctly identifies that reporting obligations apply when criteria are not met.
Note: Rule SR-MR-044-C-1 requires 'exception' case type which is not present in this batch.

  • The transaction otherwise follows margin lending structure

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-D-1-positive-01
Title: Financing trade category used for valid securities or commodities lending
Intent: Verify that a securities or commodities lending transaction meeting the specified criteria can use the Financing trade category without triggering transaction reporting obligations
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-D-1
Given
  • A securities or commodities lending transaction
  • A counterparty transfers commodities to another counterparty
  • There is a commitment that the borrower will return equivalent commodities on a future date or when requested by the transferor
When
  • The Financing trade category is assigned to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction does not result in any transaction reporting obligations under MiFID II
Assumptions
  • The transaction price is likely to be away from the prevailing current market price
Evidence

MR-044-D-1: securities financing transactions are not considered to be reportable transactions (p.14)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-044-D-1-exception-01
Title: Financing transaction at prevailing market price does not qualify
Intent: Verify that a transaction with prices at prevailing market price does not qualify for Financing trade category treatment
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-044-D-1
Given
  • A transaction that structurally meets one of the financing criteria (a), (b), (c), or (d)
  • The transaction prices are at the prevailing current market price, not away from it
When
  • The Financing trade category is assigned to the transaction
Then
  • The transaction may not qualify for the Financing trade category treatment
  • The transaction may be subject to transaction reporting obligations
Assumptions
  • The system validates whether prices are away from prevailing market price
  • The definition of 'away from prevailing current market price' requires further specification

MR-044-D-1: The Financing trade category is used for financing transactions where the prices are likely to be away from the prevailing current market price (p.14)

  • The Matching System is available for registration
When

Then
  • The Give-Up trade registration is rejected
  • An error is returned indicating the requirement for a trade concluded on an Execution Venue
Assumptions
  • The system validates the existence of a related trade on an Execution Venue before accepting registration

Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-046-02
Given
  • A Client order has been executed
  • The execution time is recorded
When
  • The executing Member enters a Give-Up Executor trade half exactly 10 minutes from the time of Execution
Then
  • The deadline validation result is accepted or rejected according to system configuration
Assumptions
  • The boundary condition at exactly 10 minutes depends on whether the deadline is inclusive or exclusive

The semantic rule implies a strict deadline ('Within 10 minutes'), suggesting a definitive pass/fail criteria should be defined.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-049-01-negative-01
Title: Executing Member fails to enter UNA mnemonic in counterparty field
Intent: Verify that the system rejects or flags incorrect counterparty field entry when using the UNA process
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-049-01
Given
  • An executing Member is using the UNA process
When
  • The executing Member enters trade data with a value other than 'UNA' in the counterparty field
Then
  • The trade data is rejected or flagged for incorrect counterparty field entry
Assumptions 
Evidence
MR-049-01: the executing Member must enter the member mnemonic “UNA” in the counterparty field (p.16)

Case Type Accepted: True

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-051-01-positive-01
Title: Executing Member cancels or reverses cleared trade when Clearing Member is known
Intent: Verify that an executing member is permitted to cancel or reverse a cleared trade when the Clearing Member identity is known
Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-051-01
Given
  • A cleared trade exists in the system
  • The Clearing Member is known
When
  • The executing Member initiates a cancel or reverse action on the cleared trade
  • The executing Member follows the steps outlined in paragraph 52
Then
  • The cancel or reverse action is permitted
  • The cleared trade is processed for cancellation or reversal
Assumptions
  • The steps in paragraph 52 are available for execution
  • The executing Member has appropriate authorization to perform the action
Evidence

Title: Clearing Member submits Give-Up Executor trade half with known Clearing Member mnemonic
Intent: Verify the workflow for canceling a cleared trade with UNA mnemonic and submitting a Give-Up Executor trade half with the known Clearing Member mnemonic
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-052-A-1
Given
  • A cleared trade exists that was created through a system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half with UNA member mnemonic
  • The Clearing Member mnemonic is now known
  • An appropriate Client reference is known
When
  • The Clearing Member cancels the cleared trade with the UNA member mnemonic
  • The Clearing Member submits a Give-Up Executor trade half with the known Clearing Member mnemonic in the Counterparty field
  • The Clearing Member includes the appropriate Client reference
Then
  • The cleared trade with UNA member mnemonic is canceled
  • The Give-Up Executor trade half is successfully submitted
  • The workflow progression is completed
Assumptions
  • The UNA member mnemonic refers to an unidentified or unknown clearing member placeholder
  • The system supports cancellation of system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade halves

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-052-A-1-exception-01
Title: Give-Up Executor trade half submission proceeds when Client reference is not known
Intent: Verify that the workflow can proceed even when the Client reference is not available, as the rule specifies it is only required 'where known'
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-052-A-1
Given
  • A cleared trade exists that was created through a system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half with UNA member mnemonic
  • The Clearing Member mnemonic is now known
  • The Client reference is not known
When
  • The Clearing Member cancels the cleared trade with the UNA member mnemonic
  • The Clearing Member submits a Give-Up Executor trade half with the known Clearing Member mnemonic in the Counterparty field
  • No Client reference is included in the submission
Then
  • The cleared trade with UNA member mnemonic is canceled
  • The Give-Up Executor trade half is successfully submitted without a Client reference
  • The workflow progression is completed
Assumptions
  • The phrase 'where known' indicates the Client reference is optional when not available
  • The system accepts submissions without Client reference in this scenario
Evidence
MR-052-A-1: with an appropriate Client reference where known (p.16)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-052-B-1
Given
  • An original Give-Up Executor trade half exists with UNA member mnemonic
  • The Clearing Member mnemonic is now known
