  • The clearing member submits a Give-Up Executor trade half with the Clearing Member mnemonic in the Counterparty field
  • The clearing member includes an appropriate Client reference
Then
  • The reversal of the original trade half is processed
  • The new Give-Up Executor trade half is accepted with the Clearing Member mnemonic
  • The workflow progresses to permitted_branch_completed state
Assumptions
  • The UNA member mnemonic is valid for reversal
  • The Clearing Member mnemonic is valid for submission

SR-MR-055-A-1TC-SR-MR-055-A-1-exception-01Execution Time Consistency for Give-Up Executor Trade Halfmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-055-A-1-exception-01
Title: Handling when original cleared trade Execution Time is unavailable
Intent: Verify system behavior when the original cleared trade Execution Time cannot be retrieved for comparison
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-055-A-1
Given
  • A Give-Up Executor trade half is being entered
  • The original cleared Give-Up Executor/Give-Up Clearer trade record is inaccessible or corrupted
  • The original Execution Time cannot be determined
When
  • The actor attempts to enter the Give-Up Executor trade half
Then
  • The system handles the exception gracefully
  • An appropriate error or warning is raised regarding the inability to validate Execution Time
Assumptions
  • The system has a defined behavior for when the original trade data is unavailable
Evidence
MR-055-A-1: the Execution Time of the Give-Up Executor trade half must be the same as that entered on the original cleared Give-Up Executor/Give-Up Clearer trade (p.17)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence quote supports the mandatory matching requirement but does not explicitly define system behavior for missing original trade data (exception handling). Test design assumes graceful degradation which is reasonable but not explicitly sourced from the provided rule text.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-056-A-1-positive-01
Title: Executing Member chooses workflow option after trade detail amendment
Intent: Verify that when trade details change, the executing Member may choose to follow either option (a) or (b) as permitted
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-056-A-1
Given
  • An original trade exists
  • The details of the original trade have changed (e.g., Prompt Date, price, volume)
When
  • The executing Member chooses to follow either option (a) or (b) as specified in the Give-Up rules
Then
  • The workflow proceeds according to the selected option
Assumptions
  • Options (a) and (b) refer to the workflow branches defined in preceding rules
Evidence
MR-056-A-1: Where the details of the original trade have changed – e.g. Prompt Date, price, volume – then the executing Member may again choose to follow either(a) or(b) as above. (p.17)

Case Type Accepted: True

Title: Executing Member fails to follow permitted workflow after trade amendment
Intent: Verify that failure to follow the permitted workflow options when trade details change results in rejection
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-056-A-1
Given
  • An original trade exists
  • The details of the original trade have changed (e.g., Prompt Date, price, volume)
When
  • The executing Member does not follow either option (a) or (b) as specified in the Give-Up rules
Then
  • The workflow does not proceed and the trade is rejected or flagged for correction
Assumptions
  • The system validates that one of the permitted workflow options is followed
Evidence
MR-056-A-1: the executing Member may again choose to follow either(a) or(b) as above. (p.17)

Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-058-01
Given
  • A give-up could not be effected on trade date T
  • A Give-Up Executor trade half has been submitted
  • A system-generated Give-Up Clearer trade half exists
When

Then
  • The Cleared Trade is processed by the Clearing House together with all other Cleared Trades

  • The Give-Up Executor trade half and Give-Up Clearer trade half have been properly matched

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-01-positive-01
Title: Give-Up Submitted Before T+1 Deadline Accepted
Intent: Verify that a Give-Up submitted before 10:00 London time on T+1 is accepted for processing
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-01
Given
  • A Give-Up has not been processed on trade date T
  • The current date is T+1
  • The current time is before 10:00 London time
When
  • The Give-Up is submitted for processing
Then
  • The Give-Up is accepted for processing
  • The deadline validation result is pass
Assumptions
  • London time refers to Europe/London timezone
Evidence
MR-059-01: The deadline for processing Give-Ups that have not been processed on T, is 10:00(London time) on T+1 (p.18)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-01-boundary-01
Title: Give-Up Submitted Exactly at T+1 Deadline Boundary
Intent: Verify the deadline boundary behavior when Give-Up is submitted at exactly 10:00 London time on T+1
Priority: high
Case Type: boundary
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-01
Given
  • A Give-Up has not been processed on trade date T
  • The current date is T+1
  • The current time is exactly 10:00 London time
When
  • The Give-Up is submitted for processing
Then
  • The deadline validation result is determined
Assumptions
  • The boundary condition at exactly 10:00 London time should be validated against system implementation
  • It is assumed that submission at exactly the deadline time may be accepted or rejected based on Clearing House policy
Evidence
MR-059-01: The deadline for processing Give-Ups that have not been processed on T, is 10:00(London time) on T+1 (p.18)
Case Type: boundary
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-059-03-positive-01
Title: Member Provides Additional Information When Requested by Exchange
Intent: Verify that a Member may provide additional information to the Exchange to demonstrate why a Give-Up could not be processed on T
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-059-03
Given
  • A Give-Up was not processed on trade date T
  • The Exchange has requested additional information from the Member
When
  • The Member provides the requested additional information to the Exchange
Then
  • The Member has fulfilled the obligation to demonstrate why the Give-Up could not be processed on T
  • The additional information is accepted by the Exchange
Assumptions
  • The Member may be required to provide information at the Exchange's discretion
  • The scope and format of additional information is determined by the Exchange
Evidence
MR-059-03: Members should be aware that they may be required to provide the Exchange with such additional information as the Exchange may request from time to time to demonstrate why it was not possible to process the Give-Up on T (p.18)

Then
  • The trade is treated in the same way as any other Agreed Trade made in the inter-office market

Title: OTC Bring-On trade not treated equivalently to standard Agreed Trade

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-061-01
Given
  • A Member has agreed to a Contingent Agreement to Trade
  • The trade is categorized as OTC Bring-On
When
  • The Member submits the Agreed Trade with the OTC Bring-On category
  • The system processes the trade differently from standard Agreed Trades
Then
  • The trade processing deviation is identified and flagged as non-compliant with equivalence requirement
Assumptions
  • The negative case tests system behavior when the equivalence requirement is violated

MR-061-01: An Agreed Trade that is submitted with the OTC Bring-On category shall therefore be treated in the same way as any other Agreed Trade made in the inter-office market. (p.18)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-062-02-positive-01
Title: Exchange and Clearing House operate without responsibility for original OTC contract
Intent: Verify that the Exchange and Clearing House are permitted to operate without responsibility or liability for the original OTC contract
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-062-02
Given
  • An original over-the-counter contract exists between Members
  • A Contingent Agreement to Trade has been submitted
When
  • The Exchange or Clearing House processes the related Agreed Trade without assuming responsibility for the original OTC contract
Then
  • Neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House has responsibility or liability to any person in respect of the original over-the-counter contract
Assumptions
  • This positive case describes the permitted state where Exchange and Clearing House are explicitly excluded from liability for the original OTC contract
Evidence
MR-062-02: Neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House shall have any responsibility or liability to any person in respect of the original over-the-counter contract. (p.18)

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-063-01-positive-01
Title: Clearing House offers OTC Backloading Service for multiple executed OTC contracts
Intent: Verify that the Clearing House may offer services to enable Members to clear multiple previously executed OTC contracts
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-063-01
Given
  • Multiple previously executed over-the-counter contracts exist
  • Members seek to clear these contracts through the Clearing House
When
  • The Clearing House offers an OTC Backloading Service to enable clearing of the multiple previously executed OTC contracts
Then
  • Members are enabled to clear through the Clearing House Cleared Contracts representing the multiple previously executed OTC contracts
Assumptions 
Evidence

  • Both counterparties have access to their respective trading systems
When

Then

  • The transaction proceeds to the next processing stage

  • The booking action by each counterparty is independent but must both complete for the transaction to be considered fully booked

  • The specific format for providing details is not specified in the evidence

  • A relevant Client Contract exists for the taken off contract
When
  • The member fails to provide full details of the relevant Client Contract
  • Or the member provides incomplete or missing Client Contract details
Then

Title: Exchange and Clearing House process OTC contract without incurring liability

Priority: medium
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-068-01
Given
  • An over-the-counter contract exists
  • The contract is processed by the Exchange or Clearing House
When
  • The Exchange or Clearing House processes the over-the-counter contract
Then
  • Neither the Exchange nor the Clearing House shall have any responsibility or liability to any person in respect of the over-the-counter contract
Assumptions
  • The positive case infers the permitted operational state from the explicit prohibition of liability

展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-069-03-positive-01
Title: TOM PTT Initiating Pair with Normal category accepted when received at or before 12:30
Intent: Verify that an IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM using the Normal trade category is accepted when received at or before 12:30
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-069-03
Given
  • An IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM is prepared
  • The trade category is set to 'Normal'
When
  • Either leg of the IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM is received at or before 12:30
Then
  • The submission is accepted by the Matching System
Assumptions
  • The positive case infers acceptance from the explicit rejection condition for submissions after 12:30
Evidence
MR-069-03: If either leg of an IOM PTT Initiating Pair for TOM has been received after 12:30 using the 'Normal' trade category the Matching System will automatically reject the submission. (p.19)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Maker evidence is weak for a positive acceptance case. The cited rule (MR-069-03) explicitly defines rejection criteria ('after 12:30 ... reject'), not acceptance criteria. The test case infers acceptance from the absence of rejection conditions, which introduces hallucination risk if other rejection criteria exist.

  • The Price Type is set to "Current"
  • The Venue is set to "Inter-Office"

Then

SR-MR-070-04TC-SR-MR-070-04-negative-01Auction Bid/Offer Submission Requirementsmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-070-04-negative-01
Title: Submit auction bid with incorrect attribute values
Intent: Verify that auction bids/offers are rejected when required attributes do not match specified values
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-070-04
Given
  • A counterparty is submitting auction bids/offers
  • At least one of the following is incorrect: counterparty is not "AUC", Category is not "Normal", Price Type is not "Current", or Venue is not "Inter-Office"
When
  • The auction bid/offer is submitted
Then
  • The submission is rejected
Assumptions
  • The rejection reason indicates which attribute value was incorrect
Evidence
MR-070-04: When submitting auction bids/offers, the counterparty must be "AUC" and the Category, Price Type and Venue must be "Normal", "Current" and "Inter-Office" respectively. (p.20)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 5,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 5
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence directly supports the negative scenario. The rule explicitly mandates specific attribute values, validating rejection on mismatch is appropriate.

Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-071-A-1
Given
  • A bid or offer is being submitted
  • The Auction ID is valid and not expired
When
  • The bid or offer is submitted for entry
Then
  • The bid or offer is accepted for entry
Assumptions
  • All other validation rules for the bid/offer are satisfied
  • The Auction ID exists in the system and has not passed its expiration time

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-071-A-1-exception-01
Title: Handle Auction ID validation during system unavailability
Intent: Verify system behavior when Auction ID validation cannot be performed due to exceptional circumstances
Priority: medium
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-071-A-1
Given
  • A bid or offer is being submitted
  • The Auction ID validation service is unavailable or returns an ambiguous status
When
  • The bid or offer is submitted for entry
Then
  • The system handles the exception gracefully
  • An appropriate error or notification is returned to the submitter
Assumptions
  • The system has defined behavior for validation service unavailability
  • The bid/offer is not automatically accepted or rejected without validation
Evidence
MR-071-A-1: Any bids or offers containing the following will be rejected on entry: (a) an invalid or expired Auction ID (p.20)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 2,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Weak evidence: The quoted rule defines business validation rejection criteria, not system exception handling behavior for service unavailability.
The scenario assumes system behavior (graceful handling of validation service failure) not explicitly stated in the provided semantic rule text.
Coverage is partial as the rule does not mandate specific exception handling outcomes.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-071-B-1-exception-01
Title: Handle fixed parameters validation with missing or malformed data
Intent: Verify system behavior when fixed parameters cannot be validated due to exceptional circumstances
Priority: medium
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-071-B-1
Given
  • A bid or offer is being submitted
  • Fixed parameters are missing, malformed, or cannot be validated due to system constraints
When
  • The bid or offer is submitted for entry
Then
  • The system handles the exception gracefully
  • An appropriate error or notification is returned to the submitter
Assumptions
  • The system has defined behavior for missing or malformed fixed parameters
  • The bid/offer is not processed without valid fixed parameters
Evidence
MR-071-B-1: Any bids or offers containing the following will be rejected on entry: (b) invalid fixed parameters (p.20)
Case Type: exception
Case Type Accepted: False
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 4,
  "test_design_quality": 3
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Maker evidence cites business rejection rule (MR-071-B-1), but case_type is declared as 'exception'.
'Missing or malformed' data typically constitutes a validation failure (negative case) rather than a system exception.
Exception cases should target system resilience (e.g., service timeouts, downstream failures) rather than data validation errors covered by the rejection rule.
Maker evidence is weak for an exception classification as it references standard business logic rejection.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-072-D-1-positive-01
Title: Bids and offers with differing volumes may be matched as partial fills
Intent: Verify that the matching system permits matching of bids and offers with differing volumes, resulting in partial fills, subject to Trading Regulation 2.15.8 criteria.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-072-D-1
Given
  • A bid and an offer exist in the matching system
  • The bid volume differs from the offer volume
  • Trading Regulation 2.15.8 criteria and limitations are satisfied
When
  • The matching system matches the bid and offer based on differing volumes
Then
  • The match is permitted and executed as a partial fill
Assumptions
  • Trading Regulation 2.15.8 criteria are assumed to be met for this positive scenario
  • The matching system supports partial fill functionality
Evidence
MR-072-D-1: bids and offers may be matched based on bids and offers of differing volumes("partial fills") (p.20)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True

  • Successful bids and offers exist at auction end
When

  • Matched trades are created for submitting to clearing

Case Type: state_transition
Paragraph IDs: MR-073-01
Given
  • An auction is in progress on the Matching System
  • Successful bids and offers are present in the auction
When
  • The auction ends and the Matching System triggers automatic matching
Then
  • The state of successful bids and offers transitions to matched trades
  • The matched trades are in a state ready for submission to clearing
Assumptions
  • The state transition is automatic without manual intervention
  • The transition occurs immediately upon auction end

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-A-1-positive-01
Title: OTC Bring-On and OTC Take-Off orders are treated as Out of Scope Orders
Intent: Verify that OTC Bring-On and OTC Take-Off orders are correctly categorized and treated as Out of Scope Orders under Regulation 2.15 of the Trading Regulations.
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • An order is submitted as an OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off trade
  • The order meets the criteria described in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
When
  • The order is processed for Regulation 2.15 compliance
Then
  • The order is treated as an Out of Scope Order for Regulation 2.15 purposes
Assumptions
  • OTC Bring-On and OTC Take-Off definitions are as described in referenced paragraphs
  • The order meets all criteria specified in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
Evidence

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-A-1-negative-01
Title: Orders not meeting OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off criteria are not treated as Out of Scope
Intent: Verify that orders that do not meet the OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off criteria are not incorrectly categorized as Out of Scope Orders.
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • An order is submitted that does not meet OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off criteria
  • The order does not fall under any other Out of Scope Order category
When
  • The order is processed for Regulation 2.15 compliance
Then
  • The order is not treated as an Out of Scope Order
  • The order is subject to standard Regulation 2.15 requirements
Assumptions
  • The order fails to meet criteria specified in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
  • No other Out of Scope Order category applies to this order
Evidence
MR-074-A-1: trade categories, orders for which, are to be treated as "Out of Scope Orders" (p.21)
Case Type: negative
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False

  "test_design_quality": 5
}

Missing Aspects 
Findings
Evidence is inferential: The rule defines inclusion criteria for Out of Scope Orders; the negative case relies on logical exclusion which is valid but not explicitly quoted as a negative constraint in the source text.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-A-1-exception-01
Title: Exceptional handling when OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off order has incomplete or invalid data
Intent: Verify the system behavior when an OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off order cannot be processed due to exceptional circumstances such as missing or invalid data.
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-A-1
Given
  • An order is submitted as an OTC Bring-On or OTC Take-Off trade
  • The order has incomplete or invalid data preventing proper categorization
When
  • The order is processed for Out of Scope Order categorization
Then
  • The order is flagged for exception handling
  • The order is not automatically categorized as Out of Scope until the exception is resolved
Assumptions
  • Exception handling procedures exist for invalid or incomplete order data
  • The specific validation rules for OTC Bring-On and OTC Take-Off are defined in paragraphs 14 and 60-68
Evidence
MR-074-A-1: OTC Bring-On / OTC Take-Off(as described in paragraphs 14 and 60 – 68) (p.21)

Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: indirect
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores

  "evidence_consistency": 3,
  "requirement_coverage": 3,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Maker evidence weak: The semantic rule defines valid categories for 'Out of Scope' status but does not explicitly define exception handling logic for invalid data. The scenario assumes system behavior not strictly covered by the provided rule text.
Hallucination risk: The 'then' clause claims orders are flagged for exception handling, which is not supported by the cited rule evidence.

Findings
Maker evidence is weak. The semantic rule defines trade categories for Out of Scope status but does not explicitly specify exception handling logic for invalid data within the provided text. The test case assumes validation rules not fully evidenced in the rule snippet, increasing hallucination risk.

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-positive-01
Title: Reversal/Correction trade is treated as Out of Scope Order
Intent: Verify that a Reversal/Correction trade is correctly classified as an Out of Scope Order under Regulation 2.15
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker submits a trade order
  • The trade is a Reversal/Correction as described in paragraphs 36-42
When
  • The trade is processed for classification under Regulation 2.15 of the Trading Regulations
Then
  • The order is treated as an "Out of Scope Order"
Assumptions
  • The Reversal/Correction trade meets all criteria specified in paragraphs 36-42
  • The trade is submitted by an authorized registered intermediating broker

Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker submits a trade order
  • The trade does not meet the Reversal/Correction criteria described in paragraphs 36-42
When
  • The trade is submitted with a Reversal/Correction classification
Then
  • The order is rejected or reclassified as not qualifying for Out of Scope treatment under Reversal/Correction category
Assumptions
  • The trade is a standard trade that should be subject to normal matching rules
  • The system validates Reversal/Correction eligibility criteria

Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-074-C-1-exception-01
Title: Reversal/Correction trade with incomplete documentation
Intent: Verify handling of Reversal/Correction trade that cannot be fully validated due to exceptional circumstances
Priority: high
Case Type: exception
Paragraph IDs: MR-074-C-1
Given
  • A registered intermediating broker submits a Reversal/Correction trade order
  • The trade references a prior transaction that cannot be fully verified
When
  • The trade is processed for Out of Scope classification
Then
  • The system flags the trade for manual review or requests additional documentation before classification
Assumptions
  • The referenced prior transaction may be archived or from a legacy system
  • Exception handling procedures exist for incomplete Reversal/Correction validation

Then
  • The system flags the trade for manual determination of its exception status before classification
Assumptions
  • Some trades may have mixed characteristics that don't clearly fit Exception Reportable or Non-Reportable categories
  • Exception handling procedures exist for ambiguous exception status determination

SR-MR-075-01TC-SR-MR-075-01-positive-01OTC Bring-On Trade Category Misuse Prohibitionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-075-01-positive-01
Title: Member uses OTC Bring-On trade category for legitimate purpose
Intent: Verify that a Member may use the OTC Bring-On trade category for its intended legitimate purpose without violating the prohibition
Priority: high
Case Type: positive
Paragraph IDs: MR-075-01
Given
  • A Member submits an Inter-Office Order
  • The Member has a legitimate business purpose for using the OTC Bring-On trade category
When
  • The Member uses the OTC Bring-On trade category for its intended purpose without intent to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
Then
  • The OTC Bring-On trade category usage is permitted and the trade is processed accordingly
Assumptions
  • The positive case is inferred from the prohibition: legitimate use of OTC Bring-On for its intended purpose is permitted
  • The Member's intent is determined to be legitimate based on the trade circumstances
Evidence
MR-075-01: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category (p.21)
Case Type: positive
Case Type Accepted: True
Coverage Relevance: direct
Blocking Findings Count: 0
Is Blocking: False
Scores
{
  "evidence_consistency": 4,
  "requirement_coverage": 5,
  "test_design_quality": 4
}
Coverage Reason:
Missing Aspects 
Findings
Maker evidence quotes the prohibition rule but does not explicitly define 'legitimate purpose'; this condition is inferred from the absence of misuse conditions.
Positive case logic is sound for a prohibition rule (verifying allowed behavior), but evidence strength is slightly reduced due to inference.
SR-MR-075-01TC-SR-MR-075-01-negative-01OTC Bring-On Trade Category Misuse Prohibitionmissingcovered
展开详情
Scenario ID: TC-SR-MR-075-01-negative-01
Title: Member mis-uses OTC Bring-On to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
Intent: Verify that a Member is prohibited from mis-using the OTC Bring-On trade category to avoid Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
Priority: high
Case Type: negative
Paragraph IDs: MR-075-01
Given
  • A Member has an Inter-Office Order that should be subject to Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements
  • The Member attempts to use the OTC Bring-On trade category
When
  • The Member mis-uses the OTC Bring-On trade category in order to avoid the application of Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements and Regulation 2.15
Then
  • The misuse is detected and the action is rejected or flagged as a violation
Assumptions
  • The system or compliance monitoring can detect patterns indicative of misuse
  • Misuse determination may involve analysis of trading patterns and intent
Evidence
MR-075-01: A Member shall not mis-use the OTC Bring-On trade category in order to avoid the application to an Inter-Office Order of the Pre-Trade Transparency Requirements (p.21)
